text:
The author, @Ada_Palmer, is a historian at the University of Chicago who write "hard social science fiction" -- a category that would be useful to expand.

She also had some very early thoughtful takes on AI, including this interesting piece from 2023: https://t.co/NsGvzniDD7 https://t.co/9fsbw5lzoc

𝕏 just added "Made with AI" labels and creators are panicking that using labels will tank engagement. 

Let's find out. If this labeled post shows up in your feed, like/comment to help settle the debate once and for all. https://t.co/YbHcsHsLwA

If ethnicity exists at all, which it obviously does, then English is an ethnicity. 

This is a simple statement of fact. It is possible to be culturally English or French or Japanese, etc, but ethnically be different. The first is “software”, the other is “hardware”.

I get the enthusiasm for open weights models but what is the justification from an alignment perspective?

And here I mean both narrow alignment (models being used improperly to do l bad things) and bigger alignment (agents or even AGI that are not aligned with humans overall).
I accidentally clicked "leave this conversation" so not sure if anyone can reply now 😅 and I can't rejoin the conversation, another thing I wish X would add / fix

I spent 200 hours testing OpenClaw, trying to find the perfect setup... My biggest takeaway: 

Keep your agents focused, and build a team.
